Title: Toxoplasma gondii requires its plant-like heme biosynthesis pathway for infection

Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: De novo heme biosynthesis entry step
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Condenses glycine and succinyl-CoA to form 5-aminolevulinic acid, the first committed precursor for heme biosynthesis.
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Supported as essential entry step for de novo heme production in this parasite
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Essential prosthetic group for respiration and diverse cellular activities required for intracellular growth and acute virulence
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes heme biosynthesis, intracellular replication, plaque formation, and acute virulence; loss causes severe heme depletion and growth defects that are rescued by ALA supplementation and complementation.
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. TgALAS deletion could only be generated when exogenous ALA was supplied, indicating de novo heme production is essential. 2. ALA-starved Δalas parasites showed severe intracellular growth, replication, and plaque defects and markedly reduced heme levels. 3. Complementation restored growth and virulence, while Δalas parasites lost acute virulence in mice.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: CRISPR-Cas9 knockout; genetic complementation; ALA starvation/rescue; luciferase growth assay; replication assay; plaque assay; mouse virulence assay; heme quantification; yeast complementation
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Gene disruption abolishes acute virulence and strongly impairs growth
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Promoters: Native promoter in endogenous locus; cognate UTR-based complementation construct
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential antiparasitic drug target in de novo heme biosynthesis; pathway inhibition could suppress parasite infection
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Deletion maintained downstream pathway intact, allowing assessment of possible salvage from host intermediates; residual growth without ALA suggests limited incorporation of host heme intermediates but not enough for normal infection.

Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: Terminal step of de novo heme biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Inserts ferrous iron into protoporphyrin IX to produce heme.
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Supported as essential terminal determinant for heme production in this parasite
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Essential prosthetic group for respiration and diverse cellular activities required for intracellular growth and acute virulence
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Expressed during acute infection stage; strongly repressed by ATc in TET-OFF strain
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Required for heme accumulation and intracellular replication; strong repression causes drastic replication defect and severe heme reduction.
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Straight knockout could not be cloned even with heme supplementation, indicating essentiality. 2. ATc-mediated knockdown reduced TgFECH below detection, lowered heme to ~15% of parental levels, and caused drastic replication defects. 3. Removing ATc partially restored growth, confirming dependence on de novo heme synthesis.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Failed knockout recovery; TET-OFF knockdown; immunoblotting; replication assay; heme quantification; yeast complementation
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: ATc treatment; ALA stimulation; succinylacetone treatment; heme supplementation
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Strong repression causes severe growth defect; inability to delete suggests essentiality
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: High-value antiparasitic target because terminal heme synthesis is essential and not rescued by extracellular heme
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Toxoplasma could not be rescued by exogenous heme, consistent with lack of identifiable heme transporter.

Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: forms immediate precursor toward target product
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Oxidatively decarboxylates coproporphyrinogen III to protoporphyrinogen IX.
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Not established as rate-limiting, but functionally important
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Essential prosthetic group for respiration and diverse cellular activities required for intracellular growth and acute virulence
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes heme production, replication, plaque development, and virulence; deletion reduces heme by ~75% and abolishes acute virulence.
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Δcpox parasites showed ~75% reduction in replication and severe plaque defects. 2. Total heme abundance dropped by ~75% relative to WT. 3. Deletion caused complete loss of acute virulence in mice, and extracellular heme did not rescue intracellular growth.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: CRISPR-Cas9 knockout; genetic complementation; luciferase growth assay; replication assay; plaque assay; mouse virulence assay; heme quantification; yeast complementation
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Deletion abolishes virulence and strongly impairs growth
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Promoters: Native promoter in endogenous locus; cognate UTR-based complementation construct
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Promising antiparasitic target in parasite-specific heme biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: TgCPDH may partially bypass loss of TgCPOX, possibly explaining residual heme synthesis and growth.

Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: forms immediate precursor toward target product
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Oxidizes protoporphyrinogen IX to protoporphyrin IX.
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Not established as rate-limiting; validated chemical target
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Essential prosthetic group for respiration and diverse cellular activities required for intracellular growth and acute virulence
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Expressed during acute infection stage; overexpressed ~12-fold in pTub-TgPPO strain
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes heme biosynthesis, replication, and virulence; deletion reduces heme by ~50% and causes major growth and virulence defects; chemical inhibition reduces parasite heme and growth.
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Δppo parasites exhibited ~50% reduction in replication, severe plaque defects, lower heme abundance, and complete loss of acute virulence. 2. TgPPO is phylogenetically closer to plant PPOs than mammalian orthologs. 3. Oxadiazon and improved derivatives inhibit parasite growth and reduce heme levels; Δppo mutants are less sensitive and TgPPO overexpression increases IC50, validating TgPPO as the in vivo target.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: CRISPR-Cas9 knockout; genetic complementation; overexpression under tubulin promoter; qPCR; luciferase growth assay; replication assay; plaque assay; mouse virulence assay; heme quantification; inhibitor sensitivity profiling
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Oxadiazon; oxadiazon derivative Compound 1; oxadiazon derivative Compound 2
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Deletion abolishes virulence and impairs growth; low-dose PPO inhibitors unexpectedly enhanced Δppo growth
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Promoters: Toxoplasma tubulin promoter (constitutive overexpression); native promoter in endogenous locus
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Strong therapeutic target due to plant-like divergence from host PPO and sensitivity to herbicidal inhibitors/derivatives
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Residual growth in Δppo may reflect slow non-enzymatic oxidation of protoporphyrinogen IX; inhibitor derivatives improved potency 15- to 25-fold over oxadiazon.
